    13	
    14	export default tool({
    15	  description: "Claim a ticket lane lease before write-capable work begins.",
    16	  args: {
    17	    ticket_id: tool.schema.string().describe("Ticket id to claim."),
    18	    owner_agent: tool.schema.string().describe("Agent name or role that will own the lease."),
    19	    allowed_paths: tool.schema.array(tool.schema.string()).describe("Repo-relative path prefixes this lease may edit.").optional(),
    20	    write_lock: tool.schema.boolean().describe("Whether this lease authorizes file edits. Defaults to true.").optional(),
    21	  },
    22	  async execute(args) {
    23	    const manifest = await loadManifest()
    24	    const workflow = await loadWorkflowState()
    25	    const ticket = getTicket(manifest, args.ticket_id)
    26	    const ownerAgent = args.owner_agent.trim()
    27	    const writeLock = args.write_lock !== false
    28	
    29	    if (!ownerAgent) {
    30	      throw new Error("owner_agent must not be empty.")
    31	    }
    32	    if (ticket.decision_blockers.length > 0 && ticket.status === "blocked") {
    33	      throw new Error(`Ticket ${ticket.id} is blocked and cannot be claimed until its decision blockers are resolved.`)
    34	    }
    35	    if (ticket.status === "done" || ticket.resolution_state === "superseded") {
    36	      throw new Error(`Ticket ${ticket.id} cannot be claimed because it is already closed.`)
    37	    }
    38	    for (const dependency of ticket.depends_on) {
    39	      const dependencyTicket = getTicket(manifest, dependency)
    40	      if (dependencyTicket.status !== "done") {
    41	        throw new Error(`Ticket ${ticket.id} cannot be claimed before dependency ${dependencyTicket.id} is done.`)
    42	      }
    43	      if (dependencyTicket.verification_state === "invalidated" || getTicketWorkflowState(workflow, dependencyTicket.id).needs_reverification) {
    44	        throw new Error(`Ticket ${ticket.id} cannot be claimed because dependency ${dependencyTicket.id} is no longer trusted.`)
    45	      }
    46	    }
    47	    if (writeLock && !allowsPreBootstrapWriteClaim(workflow, ticket)) {
    48	      await requireBootstrapReady(workflow, rootPath())
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    const lease = claimLaneLease(workflow, ticket, ownerAgent, args.allowed_paths || [], writeLock)
    52	    await saveWorkflowState(workflow)
    53	
    54	    return JSON.stringify(
    55	      {
    56	        claimed: true,
    57	        lease,
    58	        active_leases: workflow.lane_leases,
    59	      },
    60	      null,
    61	      2,
    62	    )
    63	  },
    64	})

   255	    return bool(re.search(r"(?m)^\s*dev\s*=", section.group(1)))

payload.message:
Current session is constrained to read-only planning, so I’m not editing in this turn. This is the exact patch set I would apply next.

<proposed_plan>
1. Patch the live runtime library, not the deleted helper module.
- Target [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L56), not the deleted `.opencode/tools/_workflow.ts`. The branch is already mid-migration: `git status` shows `_workflow.ts` deleted and [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts) as the live shared runtime.
- Extend `LaneLease` state in [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L69) with expiry metadata and add helpers to prune expired leases during load/claim/release paths. Current leases only store `claimed_at` and never expire at [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L316) and [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L623).
- Make `loadWorkflowState()` prune expired leases in memory before returning at [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L391). Persisted cleanup should then happen through normal saves or an explicit cleanup tool.
- Add lease helpers in [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L587): `isLeaseExpired`, `pruneExpiredLaneLeases`, and a structured result for removed leases.
- Adjust restart-surface rendering in [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L882) and [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L890) so expired leases do not appear in context or START-HERE.
- Keep `pending_process_verification` and `needs_reverification` visible in restart surfaces, but stop treating them as reasons to suppress truthful handoff publication. Current START-HERE already frames them as follow-up state, not a stop-the-world blocker, at [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L897) and [workflow.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/lib/workflow.ts#L918).

2. Fix bootstrap detection and make smoke-test runner parity real.
- In [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L73), replace the narrow `hasPyprojectDevExtra()` check with broader dev-dependency detection. Minimum support should cover the current optional extra shape plus the uv-native dev-group shape that the accepted review called out.
- Reuse that broader detection for both `uv sync --locked --extra dev` at [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L112) and editable pip install target selection at [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L179).
- Replace the current post-sync pytest probe with a runner-parity check. Right now bootstrap verifies `.venv/bin/pytest --version` at [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L127) and [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L195), while smoke test actually runs `python -m pytest` at [smoke_test.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/smoke_test.ts#L170). Bootstrap should verify the same runner shape it expects smoke test to use.
- Add smoke-test parity in [smoke_test.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/smoke_test.ts#L120): detect uv-managed repos via `.venv/pyvenv.cfg` as well as `uv.lock`, and detect pytest surfaces via `pyproject` config as bootstrap already does at [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L83) and [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L126).
- Keep bootstrap failure semantics in [environment_bootstrap.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/environment_bootstrap.ts#L392), but improve the note/output to distinguish “missing prerequisite/toolchain” from “dependency install/test failure” so recovery routing is deterministic.

3. Add explicit bootstrap and stale-lease recovery paths.
- Update [ticket_claim.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/ticket_claim.ts#L23) to prune expired leases before conflict checks and before bootstrap gating. That fixes the current “dead session still owns the lane forever” problem.
- Add a small explicit cleanup tool, likely [lease_cleanup.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools), that loads workflow state, prunes expired leases, optionally force-releases a matching stale ticket/owner tuple, saves workflow state, and returns removed leases. This is the missing operator escape hatch the runtime currently lacks.
- Update [stage-gate-enforcer.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/plugins/stage-gate-enforcer.ts#L54) to rely on post-prune lease state before emitting `missing_write_lease` or lane-conflict blockers.

4. Finish the closed-ticket reverification path so it is legal end to end.
- Keep `ticket_claim` closed-ticket rejection in [ticket_claim.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/ticket_claim.ts#L35). That part is correct.
- Preserve the narrow non-lease `ticket_reverify` gate in [stage-gate-enforcer.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/plugins/stage-gate-enforcer.ts#L190). The audit fixture already encodes the old deadlock shape at [smoke_test_scafforge.py](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/scripts/smoke_test_scafforge.py#L895).
- Harden [ticket_reverify.ts](/home/pc/projects/Scafforge/skills/repo-scaffold-factory/assets/project-template/.opencode/tools/ticket_reverify.ts#L75) so the legal mutation path is explicit about acceptable evidence sources and gives deterministic errors when evidence exists but is historical/superseded/wrong-ticket, instead of a single generic “not a current artifact” failure.
